task_completion.description: Open the Sea Level Pressure dataset in Panoply
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Panoply GUI is open and shows the SLP dataset (from ~/PanoplyData/) loaded in the Datasets/Sources panel.

task_completion.description: Create a 1D Zonal Mean SLP profile plot
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply plot window is visible displaying a 1D line plot. The axes or title should indicate it is a Zonal Mean of Sea Level Pressure (latitude on one axis, pressure on the other).

task_completion.description: Export the plot to the specified output folder
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal, or Panoply save dialog showing an image file (e.g., PNG, PDF) of the plot being saved to or existing in the output folder specified by the circulation_brief.txt.

task_completion.description: Export the raw CSV data array to the specified output folder
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal, or Panoply save dialog showing a CSV file of the data array being saved to or existing in the same output folder.

task_completion.description: Create a document identifying the latitudes of major pressure belts
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or document viewer showing a newly created file containing the identified latitudes of major pressure belts, saved in the specified output folder.

integrity.description: The agent used the Panoply GUI to generate the plot and data
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the Panoply Java application interface (menus, plot windows, export dialogs) rather than using terminal scripts (like Python/xarray) to bypass the tool.

integrity.description: The pressure belts document contains genuine data-derived values
integrity.visual_evidence: The document should list specific latitudes derived from the dataset (e.g., -32.5, 35.0, 2.5) rather than generic textbook descriptions or placeholder text.

integrity.description: The exported files are genuine outputs from Panoply
integrity.visual_evidence: The plot and CSV should be generated via Panoply's export functionality (visible save dialogs or progress bars), not mocked by creating empty files using terminal commands like 'touch'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset natively uses millibars (mb). The annual zonal mean Sea Level Pressure features specific key values: Southern Hemisphere Subtropical High peak at -32.5 degrees (1018.67 mb), Northern Hemisphere Subtropical High peak at 35.0 degrees (1017.48 mb), and Equatorial Trough minimum at 2.5 degrees (1010.86 mb). The VLM should check if the exported document and plot align with these specific latitudes and values.
